title: Strict Caduceus Original-Task Sequence Generalization Memory

resource_records.name: Strict Caduceus visible bundle
resource_records.source: Bundle containing the Caduceus paper, repository, Caduceus-PS checkpoint, 8 GenomicBenchmarks tasks, 18 Nucleotide Transformer tasks, and long-range eQTL metadata.
resource_records.access_notes: Reconstruct from public model/repository resources and portable dataset catalog entries. The historical bundle intentionally excluded DART-Eval conclusions and result tables.

resource_records.name: Caduceus direct probe recovery
resource_records.source: Manual recovery of a runnable Caduceus-PS environment after the initial agent reported the target model blocked.
portable_references: Caduceus-PS checkpoint source named by the model authors
resource_records.access_notes: Fresh runs should inspect available Python environments and the target repository before reporting a dependency blocker.

similarity_axes.name: exact_or_reverse_complement_identity
similarity_axes.definition: Detect exact and reverse-complement train-test sequence duplicates across original downstream tasks.
quality_gates: Treat as pre-benchmark screening unless followed by fresh behavioral probes.

similarity_axes.name: kmer_train_support
similarity_axes.definition: Measure local k-mer support of test examples from training sequences and test whether performance changes after high-support training examples are removed.
quality_gates: Run fresh downstream model or baseline per split.
Separate k-mer control behavior from Caduceus frozen-embedding behavior.
Report non-explanatory tasks alongside supported tasks.

similarity_axes.name: length_gc_profile
similarity_axes.definition: Use coarse sequence-composition and length profile as a prospective axis for Caduceus frozen-probe degradation.
quality_gates: Match or stratify labels where possible.
Do not treat small AUROC or MCC deltas as mechanism-level evidence.
Escalate to composition-regime or regulatory-context tests when stronger signals appear.

findings.claim: The original Caduceus downstream evaluation bundle contained data-level overlap concerns, including exact train-test duplicates and high local k-mer support in several tasks.
evidence: human_enhancers_ensembl had 38.02 percent duplicate test rows.
nucleotide_transformer enhancers and enhancers_types each had 25.00 percent duplicate test rows.
15-mer train-support curve was monotonic for 26 of 26 tasks.
limitations: It does not by itself establish target-model failure.

findings.claim: On nucleotide_transformer/enhancers, removing a small number of high-overlap training examples reduced frozen Caduceus probe MCC from 0.4602 to roughly 0.3924-0.4040.
evidence: Original split retained 14968 training rows and achieved MCC 0.4602.
Removing train examples with estimated test similarity >= 0.95 retained 14861 rows and achieved MCC 0.3924.
Removing train examples with estimated test similarity >= 0.75 retained 14860 rows and achieved MCC 0.4040.
limitations: Frozen-embedding logistic probe, not full fine-tuning.
Axis remains sequence-support level rather than mechanism-level regulatory insight.

findings.claim: In the fresh strict Caduceus comparison, the SPECTRA agent recovered the target Caduceus environment and produced standardized target-model artifacts, while the vanilla agent stopped at dependency blockage and surrogate analyses.
evidence: SPECTRA found an environment that could load Caduceus-PS.
SPECTRA produced audit_card.json, performance_by_overlap.csv, split_stats.csv, retraining_manifest.csv, and hypothesis scores.
Vanilla produced useful overlap and surrogate artifacts but no direct Caduceus controlled-split metrics.
limitations: The SPECTRA-tested axes were mostly non-explanatory for Caduceus frozen probes in that comparison.
This supports process quality, not unique discovery of a Caduceus failure.

reuse_guidance: For Caduceus-like sequence models, first check exact/reverse-complement overlap and k-mer support across all original tasks, but treat that as screening.
Before reporting a model blocker, inspect available micromamba environments; the historical pgt environment could load Caduceus with torch, transformers, and mamba_ssm.
Use nucleotide_transformer/enhancers as a known strict-bundle task where high-overlap removal produced a direct frozen-probe degradation.
Do not stop at k-mer support; route supported sequence-support axes toward composition, regulatory context, or external biological mechanism tests.
do_not_repeat: Do not conclude that direct Caduceus execution is blocked until local environments and the repository have been inspected.
Do not present exact overlap or 15-mer support as model-failure evidence without fresh behavioral testing.
Do not let a representative subset replace all-task screening when the task bundle is available.
Do not treat surrogate k-mer baseline degradation as Caduceus-specific behavior.
